task.title: Measuring the latency of outgoing TCP connections

context.p: The tcpconnlat utility uses eBPF features to measure the time between a sent SYN packet and the received response packet.
The TCP connection latency is the time taken to establish a connection. This typically involves the kernel TCP/IP processing and network round trip time, and not the application runtime.

step.cmd: Start measuring the latency of outgoing connections:

info.p: Each time the kernel processes an outgoing connection, tcpconnlat displays the details of the connection after the kernel receives the response packet.

step.cmd: Press Ctrl+C to stop the tracing process.
